mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month payroll growth above 100,000 persons signals robust labor market expansion, which increases consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:July 2010 payroll level at 132,266k suggests post-recession recovery momentum
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with positive employment momentum
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive payroll growth months do not produce significantly higher forward returns than negative payroll months (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate below 2.5% signals accommodative monetary policy that reduces equity risk premiums and lowers corporate borrowing costs, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as capital allocation shifts toward equities.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 2.16% as of June 2010 indicates low-rate regime
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 2.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint increase in retail sales and housing starts signals strengthening consumer and residential demand, which accelerates GDP growth expectations and equity valuations. The interaction effect is stronger than either signal alone because both confirm broad-based demand expansion.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 349,436M and HOUST at 1,771k as of May 2010 show recovery signs
failure_condition: The interaction term (RSAFS × HOUST) produces Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or joint positive months in both series do not produce significantly higher returns than single-series positive months (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index declining month-over-month signals supply-side weakness or demand destruction, which compresses profit margins and reduces forward earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as markets reprrice growth.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 117.813 in June 2010 reflects post-recession industrial recovery
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO months produce positive 5-day returns in > 60% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rising above 5.5% signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer confidence, which dampens discretionary spending and equity risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.4% in June 2010 near trigger threshold
regime_hint: labor market weakness or recession risk
failure_condition: UNRATE > 5.5% months produce positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation measured by CPI combined with elevated federal funds rate signals monetary tightening cycle that increases discount rates on future corporate cash flows and reduces equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 191.2 and FEDFUNDS at 2.16% as of May-June 2010
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation acceleration
failure_condition: Joint tightening signals produce positive 60-day returns in > 50% of instances, or the interaction term (CPIAUCSL × FEDFUNDS) produces Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth measured quarterly at levels below 2% annualized signals sub-trend economic expansion that constrains corporate revenue growth and equity earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as growth expectations reset lower.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 10,891.0B (2017$) as of Q1 2010 reflects post-crisis recovery pace
failure_condition: Sub-trend GDP quarters produce positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts rising above 1,500k units combined with retail sales growth signals broad-based demand recovery that accelerates capital expenditure and employment, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days through the multiplier effect on corporate earnings.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,771k and RSAFS at 349,436M in May 2010 indicate demand recovery
regime_hint: early-cycle expansion with synchronized demand acceleration
failure_condition: Joint demand signals produce negative 20-day returns in > 50% of instances, or the three-series interaction produces Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.
